The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection enlarged in 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162851|title=銚子塚古墳附小銚子塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated south-south west and has a total length of 108 meters, making it the third largest found in Shizuoka Prefecture. The anterior rectangular portion is 52 meters wide with a height of 4.5 meters, and the posterior domed portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters. There are traces of a surrounding ten-meter wide moat and ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Choshizuka Kofun (Iwata), kouenbu.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun'''|寺谷銚子塚古墳}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Teradani neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Iwata, Shizuoka|Iwata, Shizuoka]] in the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection enlarged in 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162851|title=銚子塚古墳附小銚子塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated south-south west and has a total length of 108 meters, making it the third largest found in Shizuoka Prefecture. The anterior rectangular portion is 52 meters wide with a height of 4.5 meters, and the posterior domed portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters. There are traces of a surrounding ten-meter wide moat and ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]] with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from [[:en:Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|Iwata Station]] on the [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)]]

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Choshizuka Kofun (Iwata), kouenbu.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun'''|寺谷銚子塚古墳}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Teradani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection enlarged in 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162851|title=銚子塚古墳附小銚子塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated south-south west and has a total length of 108 meters, making it the third largest found in Shizuoka Prefecture. The anterior rectangular portion is 52 meters wide with a height of 4.5 meters, and the posterior domed portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters. There are traces of a surrounding ten-meter wide moat and ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from {{ill|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|en|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q862970|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from {{ill|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|en|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q862970|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

kouenbu.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun'''|寺谷銚子塚古墳}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Teradani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection enlarged in 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162851|title=銚子塚古墳附小銚子塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated south-south west and has a total length of 108 meters, making it the third largest found in Shizuoka Prefecture. The anterior rectangular portion is 52 meters wide with a height of 4.5 meters, and the posterior domed portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters. There are traces of a surrounding ten-meter wide moat and ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}} with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from {{ill|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|en|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q862970|lt=Iwata Station}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Choshizuka Kofun (Iwata), kouenbu.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun'''|寺谷銚子塚古墳}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Teradani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection enlarged in 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162851|title=銚子塚古墳附小銚子塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated south-south west and has a total length of 108 meters, making it the third largest found in Shizuoka Prefecture. The anterior rectangular portion is 52 meters wide with a height of 4.5 meters, and the posterior domed portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters. There are traces of a surrounding ten-meter wide moat and ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from {{ill|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|en|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q862970|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station (Shizuoka)}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}}

region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Choshizuka Kofun (Iwata), kouenbu.jpg|right|thumb|270px|Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun'''|寺谷銚子塚古墳}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the Teradani neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Iwata, Shizuoka|en|Iwata, Shizuoka|WD=Q386651|lt=Iwata, Shizuoka|1=Iwata, Shizuoka}} in the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region|1=Tōkai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1956, with the area under protection enlarged in 1979.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162851|title=銚子塚古墳附小銚子塚古墳 |trans-title= Jōnozuka kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Teradani Chōshizuka Kofun is located on the east bank of the [[Tenryū River]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated south-south west and has a total length of 108 meters, making it the third largest found in Shizuoka Prefecture. The anterior rectangular portion is 52 meters wide with a height of 4.5 meters, and the posterior domed portion has a diameter of 58 meters and height of eight meters. There are traces of a surrounding ten-meter wide moat and ''[[fukiishi]]'' stones. The tumulus was [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1880 and 1898 and was found to contain a vermilion-painted vertical [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] with a length of three meters. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included an ancient triangular-edged ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} with a design of mythical animals, pottery and bronze artifacts, which dated the tomb to the middle to end of the 4th century AD.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The surrounding area contains many other ancient ''kofun'', which are not covered by the National Historic Site protection, with the exception of the '''Kochōzuka Kofun''', a smaller "two-conjoined rectangle"-shaped tomb immediately adjacent to the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tomb has a length of 54 meters, orientated east. It also has a ten-meter wide moat, which partly intersects that of the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun. This tumulus has not been excavated, and its interior details is unknown, but it is believed to predate the Teradani Chōzuka Kofun.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is a ten-minute walk from the "Choshi Tsukaguchi" bus stop on the Entetsu Bus from {{ill|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|en|Iwata Station (Shizuoka)|WD=Q862970|lt=Iwata Station|1=Iwata Station (Shizuoka)}} on the {{ill|Tōkaidō Main Line|en|Tōkaidō Main Line|WD=Q1190152|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|WD=Q6573285|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shizuoka)}}
